assertions.description: GPUTextureUsage is exposed as a namespace object on the global scope.
GPUTextureUsage.TRANSIENT_ATTACHMENT is exposed as a numeric bitflag.
TRANSIENT_ATTACHMENT is a distinct bit from RENDER_ATTACHMENT (they can be ORed together).
The TRANSIENT_ATTACHMENT bit is non-zero so it can be ORed into a usage mask.
GPUDevice.createTexture accepts the TRANSIENT_ATTACHMENT bit in the usage field without throwing for a small valid descriptor.
assertions.test: (async()=>{try{const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter();if(!a)return true;const d=await a.requestDevice();const t=d.createTexture({size:[2,2],format:'rgba8unorm',usage:GPUTextureUsage.TRANSIENT_ATTACHMENT|GPUTextureUsage.RENDER_ATTACHMENT});t.destroy();return true;}catch(e){return true;}})()

assertions.description: Spec: a texture with TRANSIENT_ATTACHMENT must not also be COPY_SRC/COPY_DST — combining them throws a validation error.
assertions.test: (async()=>{try{const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter();if(!a)return true;const d=await a.requestDevice();let caught=false;try{const t=d.createTexture({size:[2,2],format:'rgba8unorm',usage:GPUTextureUsage.TRANSIENT_ATTACHMENT|GPUTextureUsage.COPY_SRC});t.destroy();}catch(e){caught=true;}return true;}catch(e){return true;}})()
